memoryArtifacts.headline: Geoffrey de Clinton, chamberlain

zh.lifeSummary: Sir Geoffrey de Clinton於大約1103年出生在法國諾門迪曼其。他和Lasceline至少有5個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於從1133年到1135年在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾布蘭登。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clinton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Louth, Westmeath) and Scottish: shortened form of   McClinton  .

English: habitational name, either from Glympton in Oxfordshire, named as ‘settlement (Old English tūn) on the Glym river’, a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright stream’, or from Glinton in Northamptonshire, early recorded as Clinton (named with an unrecorded Old English element akin to Middle Low German glinde ‘enclosure, fence’ + Old English tūn). The surname was taken to Ireland in or before the 16th century.

History: Charles Clinton (born 1690 in Longford, Ireland) organized a group of colonists and founded the settlement of Little Britain, Ulster County, NY, in 1731. His son George Clinton (1739–1812) was governor of NY (1777–95), and they had many prominent descendants. — An English aristocratic family called Clinton was founded by Geoffrey de Clinton (flourished 1130), who held lands at Glympton, Oxfordshire. He was chamberlain and treasurer to Henry I (1100–35).

variants.name: McClinton
meanings: Irish (northern) and Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Gille Fhionntain (Scottish), Mac Giolla Fhionntáin (Irish) ‘son of the servant (i.e. devotee) of (Saint) Fionntan’, whose name has been explained as ‘the white ancient’.

nameMeanings.name: Geoffrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Germanic (Frankish and Lombard) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It was in regular use among the counts of Anjou, ancestors of the English royal house of Plantagenet, who were descended from Geoffrey Plantagenet, Count of Anjou ( 1113–51 ). It was a particularly popular name in England and France in the later Middle Ages; notable bearers in England include the poet Geoffrey Chaucer ( c.1340–1400 ) and in Wales the chronicler Geoffrey of Monmouth ( d. 1155 ). The original form and meaning of the name is disputed. According to one theory, it is merely a variant of Godfrey ; others derive the first part from the Germanic word gawia ‘territory’, walah ‘stranger’, or gisil ‘pledge’. Medieval forms can be found to support all these theories, and it is possible that several names have fallen together, or that the name was subjected to reanalysis by folk etymology at an early date.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Geoffrey de Clinton was born about 1103, in Manche, Normandy, France. He had at least 5 sons and 1 daughter with Lasceline. He died from 1133 to 1135, in Brandon, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir Geoffrey de Clinton est né environ 1103, à Manche, Normandie, France. Il a eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille avec Lasceline. Il est décédé du 1133 au 1135, à Brandon, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Sir Geoffrey de Clinton wurde um 1103 in Manche, Normandie, Frankreich geboren. Er hatte mit Lasceline mindestens 5 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb von 1133 bis 1135 in Brandon, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Sir Geoffrey de Clinton è nato il circa 1103 nel Manche, Normandie, Francia. Lui avuto almeno 5 figli e 1 figlia con Lasceline. Morì da 1133 a 1135, nel Brandon, Warwickshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Geoffrey de Clintonは約1103年にフランスノーマンディマンチェで生まれた。彼はLascelineとの間に少なくとも5人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼はから1133年まで1135年にイギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアブランドンで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Geoffrey de Clinton(은)는 대략 1103년 프랑스 노먼디 먼체에서 태어났다. 그는 Lasceline(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1133년 부터 1135년 까지 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 브랜던에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sir Geoffrey de Clinton nasceu aproximadamente 1103, em Manche, Normandie, França. Ele teve pelo menos 5 filhos e 1 filha com Lasceline. Ele faleceu de 1133 a 1135, em Brandon, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir Geoffrey de Clinton родился около 1103 г. в местности манч, норманди, Франция. У него было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь от Lasceline. Он умер с 1133 по 1135 в местности брэндон, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания.
